summary: vite-tsconfig ships a remote-code dropper disguised as Express middleware
details: Package name closely mirrors the popular vite-tsconfig-paths / tsconfig-paths libraries and replicates much of their API surface, but the exported configJson() function is a covert dropper. When a consumer calls configJson(), lib/config-loader.js spawns a detached `node lib/mapProps.js` child with stdio:'ignore' and child.unref() to hide its activity, then returns a no-op (req,res,next)=>next() middleware so callers see normal behavior. The detached child (lib/mapProps.js) performs `axios.get(src)` against a remote URL and executes the response body via `new Function('require', response.data.Cookie)(require)` — arbitrary remote JavaScript run with full Node `require` access. The fetch/exec is wrapped in a 5-retry loop. Any application that imports this package and invokes the documented configJson API silently downloads and runs attacker-controlled code on the host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
